22 CFR 121.1 Category IV(a).text: Rockets (including ballistic missiles, space launch vehicles and sounding rockets), bombs, and mines, as follows: (1) Rockets, bombs, and mines; (2) Launch vehicles and missiles capable of delivering at least a 500 kg payload to a range of at least 300 km; (3) Apparatus, devices, and materials for the above.

22 CFR 121.1 Category IV(d).text: Launch vehicles and missiles and anti-missile missiles incorporating any of the following: (1) Warheads, warhead sections, or reentry vehicles; (2) Guidance sets; (3) Thrust vector control systems; (4) Safing, arming, and fuzing systems.

22 CFR 121.1 Category IV(h); 22 CFR 120..text: Parts, components, accessories, attachments, and associated equipment specially designed (see 22 CFR 120.41) for articles in paragraphs (a) through (g) of this category, including but not limited to: rocket motor cases, nozzles, stages; combustion chambers, turbopumps, injectors; thrust vector control mechanisms; inertial measurement units specially designed for Category IV articles.

22 CFR 121.1 Category IV(h)(1).text: Propellants and their precursors, including liquid, solid, and hybrid propellants specially formulated for USML Category IV articles. Includes hydrazine (CAS 302-01-2), monomethylhydrazine (MMH, CAS 60-34-4), unsymmetrical dimethylhydrazine (UDMH, CAS 57-14-7), dinitrogen tetroxide (NTO, CAS 10544-72-6), inhibited red fuming nitric acid (IRFNA), ammonium perchlorate (AP), HMX, RDX, CL-20 (HNIW), GAP, nitromethane, and HTPB-bound composite propellants.

22 CFR 121.1 Category IV(a)(2).text: Launch vehicles and missiles capable of delivering at least a 500 kg payload to a range of at least 300 km. Aligned with MTCR Category I; threshold evaluation is numerical and applies to the complete system, not isolated components.

22 CFR 121.1 Category IV(b).text: Launch vehicle and missile sub-articles: solid, liquid, and hybrid rocket motors and engines; ramjets; scramjets; stages; interstages; and payload fairings, all specially designed for Category IV articles.

22 CFR 121.1 Category IV(c).text: Ground-support equipment for launch vehicles and missiles: transporter-erector-launchers, launch stands, umbilical towers, launch control systems, and propellant loading equipment. Note: items meeting NFPA 1122 (Code for Model Rocketry) are not USML-controlled.

22 CFR 121.1 Category IV(f).text: Production and test equipment specially designed for Category IV articles: filament winding machines, isostatic presses, rocket motor and engine test stands, industrial propellant mixers, nozzle forming equipment, and vacuum chambers for spacecraft testing.

22 CFR 121.1 Category IV(g).text: Developmental articles funded by the Department of Defense. Inclusion is triggered by DoD contract or program funding; release requires Commodity Jurisdiction determination.

CWC Schedule 1 (stub).text: Schedule 1 chemicals under the Chemical Weapons Convention. Includes nerve agents (Tabun, Sarin, Soman, VX, Novichoks), mustard agents, lewisite, saxitoxin, ricin. Structural matching against Schedule 1 substructures is a v2 feature.

CWC Schedule 1.text: Toxic chemicals on the CWC Annex on Chemicals, Schedule 1 (nerve agents, mustard agents, Novichok analogues, lewisite, saxitoxin, ricin). Possession and transfer are prohibited except for narrow research purposes under OPCW declaration.

CWC Schedule 1, Part B (precursors).text: Chemicals on CWC Schedule 1, Part B — direct precursors to Schedule 1 toxic chemicals (e.g., methylphosphonic difluoride for sarin). Subject to the same transfer prohibitions as the target agents.

CWC Schedule 2, Article VI.text: Schedule 2 chemicals are dual-use commodities that trigger declaration requirements under CWC Article VI above threshold quantities. Aggregate declaration threshold is 100 g for Schedule 2A (chemicals like amiton, BZ, PFIB).

CWC Schedule 3.text: Schedule 3 large-volume industrial chemicals with chemical-warfare potential (phosgene, cyanogen chloride, hydrogen cyanide, chloropicrin, triethanolamine). Export declarations required.

MTCR Annex (stub).text: Missile Technology Control Regime Category I items: complete rocket systems and unmanned aerial vehicle systems capable of delivering at least a 500 kg payload to a range of at least 300 km. Full range×payload threshold evaluation is a v2 feature.
MTCR Annex (stub).cfr_reference: MTCR Equipment, Software and Technology Annex

MTCR Annex Category I.text: MTCR Category I — complete rocket systems, unmanned aerial vehicle systems, and subsystems capable of delivering a payload of at least 500 kg to a range of at least 300 km. Category I items are subject to the strongest transfer presumption of denial.
MTCR Annex Category I.cfr_reference: MTCR Equipment, Software and Technology Annex

MTCR Annex Category I (UAVs).text: MTCR Category I applied to UAV systems (cruise missiles, target drones, reconnaissance drones) meeting the 500 kg / 300 km performance threshold.
MTCR Annex Category I (UAVs).cfr_reference: MTCR Equipment, Software and Technology Annex

MTCR Annex Category II.text: MTCR Category II — components, subsystems, production equipment, materials, and technology specially designed for Category I systems. Transfer is subject to case-by-case consideration.
MTCR Annex Category II.cfr_reference: MTCR Equipment, Software and Technology Annex

MTCR Annex Category II (sub-threshold).text: Sub-threshold systems (300–499 kg payload, >=300 km range) presumed controlled as Category II systems capable of WMD delivery.
MTCR Annex Category II (sub-threshold).cfr_reference: MTCR Equipment, Software and Technology Annex

Select Agents (stub).text: HHS and USDA Select Agents and Toxins list (42 CFR 73, 7 CFR 331, 9 CFR 121). Organism/toxin-level matching is a v2 feature; v1 flags for human review.

42 CFR 73.3.text: HHS Select Agents and Toxins — organisms whose possession, use, or transfer is restricted under 42 CFR 73.3 (includes Bacillus anthracis, Yersinia pestis, filoviruses, Variola spp., and enumerated select toxins).

42 CFR 73.3(d).text: HHS Select Toxins — aggregate possession at or below the enumerated thresholds (e.g., 0.5 mg botulinum toxin, 1000 mg ricin) is exempt from registration.

9 CFR 121.3.text: USDA Select Agents — organisms primarily affecting livestock (FMDV, rinderpest, classical/African swine fever) restricted under 9 CFR 121.3 (animal) and 7 CFR 331 (plant pathogens).

42 CFR 73.4 / 9 CFR 121.4.text: Overlap Select Agents — organisms under joint HHS and USDA jurisdiction (Bacillus anthracis, Brucella abortus, Burkholderia mallei, Burkholderia pseudomallei).
